I am the architect agent (read-only). I cannot write files. Let me provide the complete gap analysis report as my output, and note that writing it to disk must be handled by an executor or the user.

## Summary

After reading all key source files (~15,000+ lines across cmd.zig, server.zig, server_loop.zig, session.zig, window.zig, pane.zig, screen/screen.zig, screen/grid.zig, screen/writer.zig, screen/redraw.zig, terminal/input.zig, input_handler.zig, config/options_table.zig, render/metal.zig, render/vulkan.zig, copy/copy.zig, control/control.zig, status/format.zig), agentmux has a surprisingly deep implementation of core tmux functionality. Of the ~68 registered commands, approximately 60 have substantive implementations (not stubs). The primary gaps are: (1) several subsystems exist as data structures without integration into the event loop (control mode, popup rendering), (2) the options system is hardcoded rather than table-driven, (3) the input parser lacks UTF-8 multi-byte support and some DCS handling, and (4) the renderers (Metal/Vulkan) are structurally complete but have no windowing integration (no NSWindow/Wayland surface).

---

## 1. Stub Commands -- Registered But Handler Is Minimal/Placeholder

| Command | File:Line | Status | Notes |
|---------|-----------|--------|-------|
| `lock-client` | `cmd.zig:1023-1025` | **Stub** | Prints "client locked" -- no actual locking mechanism |
| `lock-server` | `cmd.zig:1027-1029` | **Stub** | Prints "server locked" -- no locking |
| `lock-session` | `cmd.zig:1031-1033` | **Stub** | Prints "session locked" -- no locking |
| `next-layout` | `cmd.zig:1610-1619` | **Partial** | Only calls `root.resize()` -- does not cycle through layout presets |
| `previous-layout` | `cmd.zig:1621-1630` | **Partial** | Same as next-layout -- no cycling |
| `display-popup` | `cmd.zig:3998-4028` | **Partial** | Spawns shell child synchronously -- no popup overlay rendering |
| `display-menu` | `cmd.zig:3965-3996` | **Partial** | Outputs text menu -- no interactive selection UI |
| `confirm-before` | `cmd.zig:3922-3952` | **Partial** | Outputs prompt text, but with `-y` flag only -- no interactive y/n handling routed through key events |
| `customize-mode` | `cmd.zig:3954-3963` | **Partial** | Dumps options as text -- no interactive option editor |
| `wait-for` | `cmd.zig:4126-4167` | **Partial** | Lock/signal/unlock work on channel map, but bare `wait-for channel` (blocking) returns immediately |

## 2. Missing Commands -- tmux Commands Not Registered At All

The following tmux commands are entirely absent from the `registerBuiltins()` at `cmd.zig:120-241`:

| Command | Category | Priority | Notes |
|---------|----------|----------|-------|
| `display-panes` (interactive) | Display | Medium | Registered but only lists panes textually -- no overlay digits |
| `select-pane -T title` | Pane | Low | Flag parsed at `cmd.zig:2400` but title not stored on pane |
| `select-pane -m/-M` (mark) | Pane | Low | Parsed at `cmd.zig:2398` but pane marking not implemented |
| `resize-pane -M` (mouse) | Pane | Low | Parsed but no-op |
| `capture-pane -e` (escapes) | Pane | Low | Flag parsed but escape sequences not captured |
| `capture-pane -J` (join lines) | Pane | Low | Flag parsed but not applied |
| `choose-tree` template arg | Mode | Medium | Template string ignored (positional trailing arg) |
| `command-prompt -T` types | Mode | Low | Prompt type filtering not used |
| `send-keys -F` (format) | Keys | Low | Format expansion flag parsed but not applied to key args |
| `list-clients -F` (format) | Info | Low | Custom format string not used |
| `list-sessions -F` (format) | Info | Low | Custom format string not used |
| `list-windows -F` (format) | Info | Low | Custom format string not used |
| `list-panes -F` (format) | Info | Low | Custom format string not used |

## 3. Incomplete Subsystems -- Partially Implemented Features

### 3.1 Options System (HIGH priority)

- **Location**: `config/options_table.zig:1-137`, `session.zig:33-48`, `cmd.zig:3069-3210`
- **Current state**: 16 options defined in `options_table.zig` with an `OptionsStore` that supports scoped inheritance. However, the command handlers in `cmdSetOption`/`cmdShowOptions` bypass the store entirely and use hardcoded field access on `Session.Options` and `Window.Options`.
- **Gap**: tmux has ~180 options. agentmux defines 16 in the table and only ~8 are actually settable via `set-option`. The `OptionsStore` with inheritance is built but not wired into command execution.
- **Missing options**: `default-terminal`, `escape-time`, `history-limit`, `set-clipboard`, `focus-events`, `renumber-windows`, `set-titles`, `display-time`, `pane-border-style`, `window-active-style`, `clock-mode-style`, all pane options.

### 3.2 Terminal Input Parser (MEDIUM priority)

- **Location**: `terminal/input.zig:1-458`
- **Current state**: Full state machine for CSI, OSC, ESC, DCS sequences. Byte-at-a-time parser.
- **Gap 1**: No UTF-8 multi-byte handling. `handleGround` at line 120 treats every byte >= 0x20 as a single codepoint (`@as(u21, byte)`). This means all non-ASCII characters (CJK, emoji, accented chars) are broken.
- **Gap 2**: DCS passthrough data is collected but the `dcs` event is discarded in `input_handler.zig:32` (empty match arm).
- **Gap 3**: OSC data buffer is only 256 bytes (`input.zig:79`), which truncates long OSC 52 clipboard payloads.

### 3.3 Screen Writer / CSI Handler (MEDIUM priority)

- **Location**: `input_handler.zig:1-199`, `screen/writer.zig:1-198`
- **Current state**: Handles core CSI sequences: CUP, CUU/CUD/CUF/CUB, ED, EL, IL, DL, DCH, ICH, SGR, DECSTBM, DECSET/DECRST, scroll up/down.
- **Gap 1**: `insertLines` at `writer.zig:140-152` is simplified -- it clears lines instead of actually shifting content down properly.
- **Gap 2**: `deleteLines` at `writer.zig:155-161` just clears the cursor line -- does not shift lines up within the scroll region.
- **Gap 3**: Missing CSI sequences: `X` (ECH erase characters), `b` (REP repeat), `s`/`u` (cursor save/restore via ANSI), `t` (window manipulation), `g` (tab clear).
- **Gap 4**: No handling of CSI with `>` intermediate (DA2, secondary device attributes).

### 3.4 SGR (Select Graphic Rendition) (MEDIUM priority)

- **Location**: `input_handler.zig:199+` (not fully read but SGR handler exists)
- **Current state**: Basic SGR handling for colors and attributes.
- **Gap**: Need to verify support for SGR 38/48 (256-color and RGB), SGR 58 (underline color), SGR 53 (overline), SGR 73/74/75 (super/subscript).

### 3.5 Control Mode (LOW priority)

- **Location**: `control/control.zig:1-100`
- **Current state**: `ControlClient` struct has methods for all notification types (%begin, %end, %error, %output, %session-changed, %window-add, %window-close, %window-renamed, %layout-change).
- **Gap**: Entirely unwired. No client is ever constructed as a `ControlClient`. The server's `handleIdentify` does not check for control mode flag. No notifications are sent during session/window/pane lifecycle events.

### 3.6 GPU Renderers (LOW priority for terminal mux, HIGH for GUI)

- **Location**: `render/metal.zig:0-514`, `render/vulkan.zig:0-467`
- **Current state**: Both renderers initialize GPU devices, create pipelines, manage vertex buffers, and have beginFrame/endFrame/renderCell methods. Metal uses objc_msgSend trampolines. Vulkan uses direct C API calls.
- **Gap 1**: Metal renderer's `createNSString` at `metal.zig:162-174` is broken -- returns `[NSString string]` (empty string) instead of the passed data. This means shader compilation silently fails.
- **Gap 2**: No windowing integration. Metal has no NSWindow/NSView/CAMetalLayer attachment. Vulkan has no swapchain, render pass, or framebuffer creation.
- **Gap 3**: No text rendering path from glyph atlas to screen. The `renderCell` methods exist but are never called from the server event loop.
- **Gap 4**: Sixel decoder (`render/sixel.zig`, 227 lines) and Kitty image protocol decoder (`render/kitty.zig`, 205 lines) parse image data but have no path to render the images.

### 3.7 Layout System (MEDIUM priority)

- **Location**: `layout/layout.zig`, `layout/set.zig`, `layout/custom.zig`
- **Current state**: Layout tree with horizontal/vertical splits, 5 presets (even-horizontal, even-vertical, main-horizontal, main-vertical, tiled), tmux-compatible serialization. `select-layout` applies presets correctly.
- **Gap**: `next-layout` and `previous-layout` commands do not actually cycle through presets -- they just call `resize()` on the existing root. Custom layout string parsing (from `custom.zig`) is not wired into `select-layout`.

### 3.8 Hook Execution (LOW priority)

- **Location**: `hooks/hooks.zig`, `cmd.zig:4030-4108`
- **Current state**: 19 hook types registered, hooks can be added/removed/listed. `set-hook -R` runs the hook command immediately.
- **Gap**: Hooks are never fired automatically. The server does not call `hook_registry.fire()` after session creation, window splitting, pane selection, etc. They are passive data only.

### 3.9 Status Bar Format Engine (LOW priority)

- **Location**: `status/format.zig:1-100+`, `status/status.zig`
- **Current state**: Supports `#S`, `#W`, `#I`, `#P`, `#T`, `#H`, and `#{variable_name}` long forms. ~15 format variables.
- **Gap**: tmux supports ~100+ format variables, conditionals (`#{?var,true,false}`), comparisons (`#{==:a,b}`), string operations. None of these are implemented.

## 4. Missing Subsystems -- Features Not Started At All

| Subsystem | Priority | Complexity | Notes |
|-----------|----------|------------|-------|
| **Mouse event routing** | High | Medium | Screen mode flags are set (`mouse_standard`, `mouse_button`, `mouse_any`, `mouse_sgr` in `screen.zig:25-29`), but no mouse events are parsed from client input or forwarded to panes |
| **Alt-screen buffer** | High | Medium | `mode.alt_screen` flag is set/cleared in `input_handler.zig:169-182`, but there is no second Grid backing the alt screen -- the flag is cosmetic |
| **Window/pane border drawing** | High | Medium | No border characters drawn between panes. `redraw.zig` renders a single pane's grid -- multi-pane compositing is absent |
| **Activity/bell/silence monitoring** | Medium | Low | Window flags exist (`flags.bell`, `flags.activity`, `flags.silence` in `window.zig:140-143`) but nothing sets them |
| **Automatic window renaming** | Medium | Low | No mechanism to read the foreground process name from the PTY and update `window.name` |
| **$TERM / terminfo** | Medium | Low | `terminal/terminfo.zig` and `terminal/features.zig` exist but their integration with the output path is unclear -- the server outputs raw escape sequences |
| **remain-on-exit** | Low | Low | Option defined (`window.zig:136`) but pane exit is not detected; no "Pane is dead" overlay |
| **Session groups** | Low | Medium | tmux's session grouping (shared windows) not implemented |
| **Client read-only mode** | Low | Low | ACL entries track `read_only` flag (`server.zig:26`) but enforcement is missing |
| **Extended keys (CSI u)** | Low | Medium | No support for the kitty keyboard protocol or xterm modifyOtherKeys |
| **Hyperlinks (OSC 8)** | Low | Low | Not parsed or stored in cells |
| **Synchronized output (DEC 2026)** | Low | Low | Not handled in DECSET |
| **True color (24-bit) passthrough** | Low | Low | Colour model supports RGB (`core/colour.zig`), but SGR 38;2;r;g;b parsing needs verification |

## 5. Priority Ranking -- What To Implement First

### Tier 1: Critical for Basic Usability

1. **UTF-8 multi-byte input parsing** -- Without this, any non-ASCII program output corrupts the screen. Fix `input.zig:120-122` to decode multi-byte UTF-8 before emitting `print` events. Effort: Low. Impact: High.

2. **Alt-screen buffer** -- Programs like vim, less, htop switch to the alt screen. Without a second Grid, these programs corrupt the primary scrollback. Effort: Medium. Impact: High.

3. **Multi-pane compositing / border drawing** -- Splitting panes works at the data model level, but the rendered output to the client only shows one pane's grid (`server.zig:822-824` calls `ps.renderTo(&out)` for a single pane). Each pane must be rendered at its `xoff`/`yoff` position with border characters between them. Effort: High. Impact: High.

4. **Mouse event routing** -- Many terminal programs (tmux users expect `set -g mouse on` to work) depend on mouse input. The mode flags are tracked but events are never generated or forwarded. Effort: Medium. Impact: High.

### Tier 2: Important for tmux Compatibility

5. **Wire the options store** -- Replace hardcoded option access in `cmdSetOption`/`cmdShowOptions` with the existing `OptionsStore` from `config/options.zig`. Add the ~30 most commonly used tmux options. Effort: Medium. Impact: Medium.

6. **Fix insertLines/deleteLines in writer.zig** -- Currently broken (clear instead of shift). This affects `IL`/`DL` CSI sequences used by ncurses programs. Effort: Low. Impact: Medium.

7. **Layout cycling** -- Wire `next-layout`/`previous-layout` to actually cycle through the 5 presets. Effort: Low. Impact: Medium.

8. **Hook auto-firing** -- Add `hook_registry.fire()` calls at key server lifecycle points (after createSession, addWindow, splitActivePane, selectPane, etc.). Effort: Low. Impact: Medium.

9. **Activity/bell detection** -- Check for BEL character in PTY output and set `window.flags.bell`. Effort: Low. Impact: Low.

### Tier 3: Nice-to-Have for Full Compatibility

10. **Control mode integration** -- Wire `ControlClient` into the server's client handling. Effort: Medium. Impact: Medium (needed for tmux plugins like tmuxinator, teamocil).

11. **Format engine conditionals** -- Add `#{?var,true,false}` support to `status/format.zig`. Effort: Medium. Impact: Low.

12. **Fix Metal createNSString** -- The broken NSString creation at `metal.zig:162-174` prevents shader compilation. Effort: Low. Impact: Low (only affects GUI mode).

13. **Windowing integration for renderers** -- Create NSWindow+CAMetalLayer (macOS) or Wayland/X11 surface + swapchain (Linux). Effort: High. Impact: Low (terminal mux works without GPU rendering).

14. **DCS passthrough** -- Handle DCS events in `input_handler.zig:32` for DECRQSS, tmux escape passthrough, etc. Effort: Medium. Impact: Low.

15. **Extended key protocols** -- CSI u (kitty keyboard) and modifyOtherKeys. Effort: Medium. Impact: Low.

## Trade-offs

| Option | Pros | Cons |
|--------|------|------|
| Fix input parser + alt screen first | Unblocks basic usage of vim/less/htop; small effort | Does not address multi-pane rendering -- users see only one pane |
| Multi-pane compositing first | Visual correctness for the core use case (splitting) | Large effort; depends on correct input parsing to look right |
| Wire options store first | Clean architecture, makes all other options trivial to add | No user-visible improvement until options actually affect behavior |
| GPU renderers first | Differentiating feature vs tmux | Massive effort (windowing, text shaping, etc.); terminal-mode mux works without it |

## References

- `src/terminal/input.zig:120-122` -- UTF-8 gap (single-byte print only)
- `src/input_handler.zig:32` -- DCS events discarded
- `src/input_handler.zig:169-182` -- Alt screen flag set but no second Grid
- `src/screen/writer.zig:140-161` -- Broken insertLines/deleteLines
- `src/server.zig:822-824` -- Single-pane rendering (no multi-pane compositing)
- `src/cmd/cmd.zig:1023-1033` -- Lock command stubs
- `src/cmd/cmd.zig:1610-1630` -- Layout cycling not implemented
- `src/cmd/cmd.zig:3069-3210` -- Hardcoded option handling bypasses OptionsStore
- `src/config/options_table.zig:17-137` -- Only 16 of ~180 tmux options defined
- `src/control/control.zig:1-100` -- Control mode entirely unwired
- `src/render/metal.zig:162-174` -- Broken createNSString
- `src/hooks/hooks.zig` -- Hooks never auto-fired
- `src/status/format.zig:20-100` -- No conditionals or comparisons in format engine
- `src/window.zig:140-143` -- Activity/bell/silence flags never set

| `display-panes` (interactive) | Display | Medium | Registered but only lists panes textually -- no overlay digits |
| `select-pane -T title` | Pane | Low | Flag parsed at `cmd.zig:2400` but title not stored on pane |
| `select-pane -m/-M` (mark) | Pane | Low | Parsed at `cmd.zig:2398` but pane marking not implemented |

| `capture-pane -e` (escapes) | Pane | Low | Flag parsed but escape sequences not captured |
| `capture-pane -J` (join lines) | Pane | Low | Flag parsed but not applied |

### 3.2 Terminal Input Parser (MEDIUM priority)

- **Location**: `terminal/input.zig:1-458`
- **Current state**: Full state machine for CSI, OSC, ESC, DCS sequences. Byte-at-a-time parser.
- **Gap 1**: No UTF-8 multi-byte handling. `handleGround` at line 120 treats every byte >= 0x20 as a single codepoint (`@as(u21, byte)`). This means all non-ASCII characters (CJK, emoji, accented chars) are broken.
- **Gap 2**: DCS passthrough data is collected but the `dcs` event is discarded in `input_handler.zig:32` (empty match arm).

- **Location**: `input_handler.zig:1-199`, `screen/writer.zig:1-198`

- **Location**: `input_handler.zig:199+` (not fully read but SGR handler exists)
- **Current state**: Basic SGR handling for colors and attributes.
- **Gap**: Need to verify support for SGR 38/48 (256-color and RGB), SGR 58 (underline color), SGR 53 (overline), SGR 73/74/75 (super/subscript).

### 3.5 Control Mode (LOW priority)

- **Location**: `control/control.zig:1-100`
- **Current state**: `ControlClient` struct has methods for all notification types (%begin, %end, %error, %output, %session-changed, %window-add, %window-close, %window-renamed, %layout-change).
- **Gap**: Entirely unwired. No client is ever constructed as a `ControlClient`. The server's `handleIdentify` does not check for control mode flag. No notifications are sent during session/window/pane lifecycle events.

### 3.6 GPU Renderers (LOW priority for terminal mux, HIGH for GUI)

- **Location**: `render/metal.zig:0-514`, `render/vulkan.zig:0-467`
- **Current state**: Both renderers initialize GPU devices, create pipelines, manage vertex buffers, and have beginFrame/endFrame/renderCell methods. Metal uses objc_msgSend trampolines. Vulkan uses direct C API calls.
- **Gap 1**: Metal renderer's `createNSString` at `metal.zig:162-174` is broken -- returns `[NSString string]` (empty string) instead of the passed data. This means shader compilation silently fails.
- **Gap 2**: No windowing integration. Metal has no NSWindow/NSView/CAMetalLayer attachment. Vulkan has no swapchain, render pass, or framebuffer creation.
- **Gap 3**: No text rendering path from glyph atlas to screen. The `renderCell` methods exist but are never called from the server event loop.
- **Gap 4**: Sixel decoder (`render/sixel.zig`, 227 lines) and Kitty image protocol decoder (`render/kitty.zig`, 205 lines) parse image data but have no path to render the images.

### 3.7 Layout System (MEDIUM priority)

- **Location**: `layout/layout.zig`, `layout/set.zig`, `layout/custom.zig`
- **Current state**: Layout tree with horizontal/vertical splits, 5 presets (even-horizontal, even-vertical, main-horizontal, main-vertical, tiled), tmux-compatible serialization. `select-layout` applies presets correctly.
- **Gap**: `next-layout` and `previous-layout` commands do not actually cycle through presets -- they just call `resize()` on the existing root. Custom layout string parsing (from `custom.zig`) is not wired into `select-layout`.

### 3.8 Hook Execution (LOW priority)

- **Location**: `hooks/hooks.zig`, `cmd.zig:4030-4108`
- **Current state**: 19 hook types registered, hooks can be added/removed/listed. `set-hook -R` runs the hook command immediately.
- **Gap**: Hooks are never fired automatically. The server does not call `hook_registry.fire()` after session creation, window splitting, pane selection, etc. They are passive data only.

### 3.9 Status Bar Format Engine (LOW priority)

- **Location**: `status/format.zig:1-100+`, `status/status.zig`
- **Current state**: Supports `#S`, `#W`, `#I`, `#P`, `#T`, `#H`, and `#{variable_name}` long forms. ~15 format variables.
- **Gap**: tmux supports ~100+ format variables, conditionals (`#{?var,true,false}`), comparisons (`#{==:a,b}`), string operations. None of these are implemented.

## 4. Missing Subsystems -- Features Not Started At All

| Subsystem | Priority | Complexity | Notes |
|-----------|----------|------------|-------|
| **Mouse event routing** | High | Medium | Screen mode flags are set (`mouse_standard`, `mouse_button`, `mouse_any`, `mouse_sgr` in `screen.zig:25-29`), but no mouse events are parsed from client input or forwarded to panes |
| **Alt-screen buffer** | High | Medium | `mode.alt_screen` flag is set/cleared in `input_handler.zig:169-182`, but there is no second Grid backing the alt screen -- the flag is cosmetic |
| **Window/pane border drawing** | High | Medium | No border characters drawn between panes. `redraw.zig` renders a single pane's grid -- multi-pane compositing is absent |
| **Activity/bell/silence monitoring** | Medium | Low | Window flags exist (`flags.bell`, `flags.activity`, `flags.silence` in `window.zig:140-143`) but nothing sets them |
| **Automatic window renaming** | Medium | Low | No mechanism to read the foreground process name from the PTY and update `window.name` |
| **$TERM / terminfo** | Medium | Low | `terminal/terminfo.zig` and `terminal/features.zig` exist but their integration with the output path is unclear -- the server outputs raw escape sequences |
| **remain-on-exit** | Low | Low | Option defined (`window.zig:136`) but pane exit is not detected; no "Pane is dead" overlay |
| **Session groups** | Low | Medium | tmux's session grouping (shared windows) not implemented |
| **Client read-only mode** | Low | Low | ACL entries track `read_only` flag (`server.zig:26`) but enforcement is missing |
| **Extended keys (CSI u)** | Low | Medium | No support for the kitty keyboard protocol or xterm modifyOtherKeys |
| **Hyperlinks (OSC 8)** | Low | Low | Not parsed or stored in cells |
| **Synchronized output (DEC 2026)** | Low | Low | Not handled in DECSET |
| **True color (24-bit) passthrough** | Low | Low | Colour model supports RGB (`core/colour.zig`), but SGR 38;2;r;g;b parsing needs verification |

## 5. Priority Ranking -- What To Implement First

### Tier 1: Critical for Basic Usability

1. **UTF-8 multi-byte input parsing** -- Without this, any non-ASCII program output corrupts the screen. Fix `input.zig:120-122` to decode multi-byte UTF-8 before emitting `print` events. Effort: Low. Impact: High.

2. **Alt-screen buffer** -- Programs like vim, less, htop switch to the alt screen. Without a second Grid, these programs corrupt the primary scrollback. Effort: Medium. Impact: High.

Each pane must be rendered at its `xoff`/`yoff` position with border characters between them. Effort: High. Impact: High.

4. **Mouse event routing** -- Many terminal programs (tmux users expect `set -g mouse on` to work) depend on mouse input. The mode flags are tracked but events are never generated or forwarded. Effort: Medium. Impact: High.

### Tier 2: Important for tmux Compatibility

5. **Wire the options store** -- Replace hardcoded option access in `cmdSetOption`/`cmdShowOptions` with the existing `OptionsStore` from `config/options.zig`. Add the ~30 most commonly used tmux options. Effort: Medium. Impact: Medium.

6. **Fix insertLines/deleteLines in writer.zig** -- Currently broken (clear instead of shift). This affects `IL`/`DL` CSI sequences used by ncurses programs. Effort: Low. Impact: Medium.

7. **Layout cycling** -- Wire `next-layout`/`previous-layout` to actually cycle through the 5 presets. Effort: Low. Impact: Medium.

8. **Hook auto-firing** -- Add `hook_registry.fire()` calls at key server lifecycle points (after createSession, addWindow, splitActivePane, selectPane, etc.). Effort: Low. Impact: Medium.

9. **Activity/bell detection** -- Check for BEL character in PTY output and set `window.flags.bell`. Effort: Low. Impact: Low.

### Tier 3: Nice-to-Have for Full Compatibility

10. **Control mode integration** -- Wire `ControlClient` into the server's client handling. Effort: Medium. Impact: Medium (needed for tmux plugins like tmuxinator, teamocil).

11. **Format engine conditionals** -- Add `#{?var,true,false}` support to `status/format.zig`. Effort: Medium. Impact: Low.

12. **Fix Metal createNSString** -- The broken NSString creation at `metal.zig:162-174` prevents shader compilation. Effort: Low. Impact: Low (only affects GUI mode).

13. **Windowing integration for renderers** -- Create NSWindow+CAMetalLayer (macOS) or Wayland/X11 surface + swapchain (Linux). Effort: High. Impact: Low (terminal mux works without GPU rendering).

| Option | Pros | Cons |
|--------|------|------|
| Fix input parser + alt screen first | Unblocks basic usage of vim/less/htop; small effort | Does not address multi-pane rendering -- users see only one pane |

# agentmux Full Zig Port of tmux u2014 Gap Analysis & Spec

## Current State

agentmux has ~15,000+ lines of Zig implementing a tmux-compatible terminal multiplexer with:
- Client/server over Unix domain sockets
- Wire protocol with typed messages
- ~68 registered commands (~60 with substantive implementations)
- Session/Window/Pane models with PTY management
- VT100/xterm input parser (CSI, OSC, ESC, DCS)
- Screen grid with scrollback, writer, dirty-tracked redraw
- Copy mode (vi/emacs), paste buffer stack
- Key binding manager with tables
- Layout tree with 5 presets and tmux-compatible serialization
- Config parser, option system with scoped inheritance
- Hook registry (19 event types)
- Status bar with format expansion
- Metal/Vulkan renderers, glyph atlas, FreeType, Sixel/Kitty image decoders
- Platform abstraction (GCD/io_uring event loops)

## Tier 1: Critical for Basic Usability

### 1.1 UTF-8 Multi-byte Input Parsing
- **Location**: `src/terminal/input.zig:120-122`
- **Problem**: `handleGround` treats every byte >= 0x20 as a single codepoint. Non-ASCII (CJK, emoji, accented chars) is broken.
- **Fix**: Decode multi-byte UTF-8 sequences before emitting `print` events. Accumulate continuation bytes, validate, emit u21 codepoint.
- **Effort**: Low

### 1.2 Alt-Screen Buffer
- **Location**: `src/input_handler.zig:169-182`, `src/screen/screen.zig`
- **Problem**: `mode.alt_screen` flag is toggled but there's no second Grid. Programs like vim/less/htop corrupt primary scrollback.
- **Fix**: Add `alt_grid: ?Grid` to `Screen`. On DECSET 1049, save cursor + swap grids. On DECRST 1049, restore.
- **Effort**: Medium

### 1.3 Multi-Pane Compositing & Border Drawing
- **Location**: `src/server.zig:822-824`, `src/screen/redraw.zig`
- **Problem**: Server renders only one pane's grid. Split panes exist in the data model but aren't composited to the client.
- **Fix**: Iterate all visible panes in the active window, render each at its `xoff`/`yoff`, draw border characters (box-drawing) between panes.
- **Effort**: High

### 1.4 Mouse Event Routing
- **Location**: `src/screen/screen.zig:25-29`, `src/terminal/input.zig`
- **Problem**: Mouse mode flags are tracked but no mouse events are parsed from client input or forwarded to panes.
- **Fix**: Parse SGR mouse sequences (`ESC[<btn;x;y;M/m`), route to correct pane based on coordinates, forward to pane's PTY.
- **Effort**: Medium

## Tier 2: Important for tmux Compatibility

### 2.1 Wire the Options Store
- **Location**: `src/cmd/cmd.zig:3069-3210`, `src/config/options.zig`, `src/config/options_table.zig`
- **Problem**: `cmdSetOption`/`cmdShowOptions` bypass OptionsStore with hardcoded field access. Only 16 of ~180 tmux options defined.
- **Fix**: Route set/show through `OptionsStore`. Add ~30 most common options to `options_table.zig`.
- **Effort**: Medium

### 2.2 Fix insertLines/deleteLines
- **Location**: `src/screen/writer.zig:140-161`
- **Problem**: `insertLines` clears instead of shifting content down. `deleteLines` clears cursor line instead of shifting up.
- **Fix**: Implement proper line shifting within scroll region, respecting scroll_top/scroll_bottom.
- **Effort**: Low

### 2.3 Layout Cycling
- **Location**: `src/cmd/cmd.zig:1610-1630`
- **Problem**: `next-layout`/`previous-layout` just call `resize()` instead of cycling through presets.
- **Fix**: Track current preset index per window, cycle through `LayoutPreset` variants, rebuild layout tree.
- **Effort**: Low

### 2.4 Hook Auto-Firing
- **Location**: `src/hooks/hooks.zig`, `src/server.zig`
- **Problem**: Hooks are registered but never fired. Server lifecycle events don't call `hook_registry.fire()`.
- **Fix**: Add `fire()` calls after createSession, addWindow, splitActivePane, selectPane, selectWindow, renameSession, renameWindow, client attach/detach, pane exit.
- **Effort**: Low

### 2.5 Activity/Bell Detection
- **Location**: `src/window.zig:140-143`, `src/server_loop.zig`
- **Problem**: `window.flags.bell`/`activity`/`silence` are never set.
- **Fix**: Check for BEL (0x07) in `PaneState.processPtyOutput()`, set window flags, notify via hooks.
- **Effort**: Low

### 2.6 Missing CSI Sequences
- **Location**: `src/input_handler.zig:50-199`
- **Missing**: ECH (`X`), REP (`b`), ANSI cursor save/restore (`s`/`u`), tab clear (`g`), DA2 (`>`)
- **Effort**: Low

## Tier 3: Full Compatibility

### 3.1 Control Mode Integration
- **Location**: `src/control/control.zig`
- **Problem**: Entirely unwired. No ControlClient constructed.
- **Effort**: Medium

### 3.2 Format Engine Conditionals
- **Location**: `src/status/format.zig`
- **Problem**: No `#{?var,true,false}`, `#{==:a,b}`, string operations.
- **Effort**: Medium

### 3.3 Stub Command Completion
- Lock commands (lock-client/server/session): implement actual locking
- display-popup: render popup overlay
- display-menu: interactive selection UI
- confirm-before: interactive y/n via key events
- customize-mode: interactive option editor
- wait-for: blocking wait on channel
- **Effort**: Medium-High (popup/menu require overlay rendering)

### 3.4 Metal createNSString Fix
- **Location**: `src/render/metal.zig:162-174`
- **Problem**: Returns empty string instead of the passed data.
- **Effort**: Low

### 3.5 Automatic Window Renaming
- Read foreground process name from PTY, update window.name.
- **Effort**: Low

### 3.6 Extended Key Protocols (CSI u)
- Kitty keyboard protocol, xterm modifyOtherKeys.
- **Effort**: Medium

### 3.7 Pane Title (select-pane -T)
- Store title on Pane, display in status/border.
- **Effort**: Low

### 3.8 Pane Marking (select-pane -m/-M)
- Track marked pane for `{marked}` target.
- **Effort**: Low

### 3.9 DCS Passthrough
- Handle DCS events in input_handler for DECRQSS, tmux escape passthrough.
- **Effort**: Medium

### 3.10 remain-on-exit
- Detect pane exit, show "Pane is dead" overlay.
- **Effort**: Low

### 3.11 Session Groups
- Shared windows across sessions.
- **Effort**: Medium

### 3.12 OSC Buffer Size
- **Location**: `src/terminal/input.zig:79`
- Increase OSC data buffer from 256 to 65536 for clipboard payloads.
- **Effort**: Low

- **Fix**: Add `fire()` calls after createSession, addWindow, splitActivePane, selectPane, selectWindow, renameSession, renameWindow, client attach/detach, pane exit.

- **Location**: `src/window.zig:140-143`, `src/server_loop.zig`

- **Location**: `src/input_handler.zig:50-199`

### 3.1 Control Mode Integration
- **Location**: `src/control/control.zig`
- **Problem**: Entirely unwired. No ControlClient constructed.
- **Effort**: Medium

### 3.2 Format Engine Conditionals
- **Location**: `src/status/format.zig`
- **Problem**: No `#{?var,true,false}`, `#{==:a,b}`, string operations.
- **Effort**: Medium

### 3.3 Stub Command Completion
- Lock commands (lock-client/server/session): implement actual locking
- display-popup: render popup overlay
- display-menu: interactive selection UI
- confirm-before: interactive y/n via key events
- customize-mode: interactive option editor
- wait-for: blocking wait on channel
- **Effort**: Medium-High (popup/menu require overlay rendering)

### 3.4 Metal createNSString Fix
- **Location**: `src/render/metal.zig:162-174`
- **Problem**: Returns empty string instead of the passed data.
- **Effort**: Low

### 3.5 Automatic Window Renaming
- Read foreground process name from PTY, update window.name.
- **Effort**: Low

### 3.6 Extended Key Protocols (CSI u)
- Kitty keyboard protocol, xterm modifyOtherKeys.
- **Effort**: Medium

### 3.7 Pane Title (select-pane -T)
- Store title on Pane, display in status/border.
- **Effort**: Low

### 3.8 Pane Marking (select-pane -m/-M)
- Track marked pane for `{marked}` target.
- **Effort**: Low

### 3.9 DCS Passthrough
- Handle DCS events in input_handler for DECRQSS, tmux escape passthrough.
- **Effort**: Medium

### 3.10 remain-on-exit
- Detect pane exit, show "Pane is dead" overlay.
- **Effort**: Low

### 3.11 Session Groups
- Shared windows across sessions.
- **Effort**: Medium

### 3.12 OSC Buffer Size
- **Location**: `src/terminal/input.zig:79`
- Increase OSC data buffer from 256 to 65536 for clipboard payloads.
- **Effort**: Low

1. UTF-8 input parsing (1.1) — unblocks all non-ASCII
2. Fix insertLines/deleteLines (2.2) — unblocks ncurses apps
3. Alt-screen buffer (1.2) — unblocks vim/less/htop
4. Missing CSI sequences (2.6) — completes terminal emulation
5. Multi-pane compositing (1.3) — core visual feature
6. Mouse event routing (1.4) — expected by users
7. Hook auto-firing (2.4) — low effort, high value
8. Layout cycling (2.3) — low effort
9. Activity/bell detection (2.5) — low effort
10. OSC buffer size (3.12) — low effort
11. Wire options store (2.1) — architectural cleanup
12. Control mode (3.1) — enables tooling integration
13. Format conditionals (3.2) — status bar power
14. Stub commands (3.3) — full command parity
15. Remaining Tier 3 items

# agentmux Full Zig Port of tmux — Gap Analysis & Spec

Generated: 2026-04-06

## Current State

agentmux has ~15,000+ lines of Zig implementing a tmux-compatible terminal multiplexer with:
- Client/server over Unix domain sockets
- Wire protocol with typed messages
- ~68 registered commands (~60 with substantive implementations)
- Session/Window/Pane models with PTY management
- VT100/xterm input parser (CSI, OSC, ESC, DCS)
- Screen grid with scrollback, writer, dirty-tracked redraw
- Copy mode (vi/emacs), paste buffer stack
- Key binding manager with tables
- Layout tree with 5 presets and tmux-compatible serialization
- Config parser, option system with scoped inheritance
- Hook registry (19 event types)
- Status bar with format expansion
- Metal/Vulkan renderers, glyph atlas, FreeType, Sixel/Kitty image decoders
- Platform abstraction (GCD/io_uring event loops)

## Tier 1: Critical for Basic Usability

### 1.1 UTF-8 Multi-byte Input Parsing
- **Location**: `src/terminal/input.zig:120-122`
- **Problem**: `handleGround` treats every byte >= 0x20 as a single codepoint. Non-ASCII (CJK, emoji, accented chars) is broken.
- **Fix**: Decode multi-byte UTF-8 sequences before emitting `print` events. Accumulate continuation bytes, validate, emit u21 codepoint.
- **Effort**: Low

### 1.2 Alt-Screen Buffer
- **Location**: `src/input_handler.zig:169-182`, `src/screen/screen.zig`
- **Problem**: `mode.alt_screen` flag is toggled but there's no second Grid. Programs like vim/less/htop corrupt primary scrollback.
- **Fix**: Add `alt_grid: ?Grid` to `Screen`. On DECSET 1049, save cursor + swap grids. On DECRST 1049, restore.
- **Effort**: Medium

### 1.3 Multi-Pane Compositing & Border Drawing
- **Location**: `src/server.zig:822-824`, `src/screen/redraw.zig`
- **Problem**: Server renders only one pane's grid. Split panes exist in the data model but aren't composited to the client.
- **Fix**: Iterate all visible panes in the active window, render each at its `xoff`/`yoff`, draw border characters (box-drawing) between panes.
- **Effort**: High

### 1.4 Mouse Event Routing
- **Location**: `src/screen/screen.zig:25-29`, `src/terminal/input.zig`
- **Problem**: Mouse mode flags are tracked but no mouse events are parsed from client input or forwarded to panes.
- **Fix**: Parse SGR mouse sequences (`ESC[<btn;x;y;M/m`), route to correct pane based on coordinates, forward to pane's PTY.
- **Effort**: Medium

## Tier 2: Important for tmux Compatibility

### 2.1 Wire the Options Store
- **Location**: `src/cmd/cmd.zig:3069-3210`, `src/config/options.zig`, `src/config/options_table.zig`
- **Problem**: `cmdSetOption`/`cmdShowOptions` bypass OptionsStore with hardcoded field access. Only 16 of ~180 tmux options defined.
- **Fix**: Route set/show through `OptionsStore`. Add ~30 most common options to `options_table.zig`.
- **Effort**: Medium

### 2.2 Fix insertLines/deleteLines
- **Location**: `src/screen/writer.zig:140-161`
- **Problem**: `insertLines` clears instead of shifting content down. `deleteLines` clears cursor line instead of shifting up.
- **Fix**: Implement proper line shifting within scroll region, respecting scroll_top/scroll_bottom.
- **Effort**: Low

### 2.3 Layout Cycling
- **Location**: `src/cmd/cmd.zig:1610-1630`
- **Problem**: `next-layout`/`previous-layout` just call `resize()` instead of cycling through presets.
- **Fix**: Track current preset index per window, cycle through `LayoutPreset` variants, rebuild layout tree.
- **Effort**: Low

### 2.4 Hook Auto-Firing
- **Location**: `src/hooks/hooks.zig`, `src/server.zig`
- **Problem**: Hooks are registered but never fired. Server lifecycle events don't call `hook_registry.fire()`.
- **Fix**: Add `fire()` calls after createSession, addWindow, splitActivePane, selectPane, selectWindow, renameSession, renameWindow, client attach/detach, pane exit.
- **Effort**: Low

### 2.5 Activity/Bell Detection
- **Location**: `src/window.zig:140-143`, `src/server_loop.zig`
- **Problem**: `window.flags.bell`/`activity`/`silence` are never set.
- **Fix**: Check for BEL (0x07) in `PaneState.processPtyOutput()`, set window flags, notify via hooks.
- **Effort**: Low

### 2.6 Missing CSI Sequences
- **Location**: `src/input_handler.zig:50-199`
- **Missing**: ECH (`X`), REP (`b`), ANSI cursor save/restore (`s`/`u`), tab clear (`g`), DA2 (`>`)
- **Effort**: Low

## Tier 3: Full Compatibility

### 3.1 Control Mode Integration
- **Location**: `src/control/control.zig`
- **Problem**: Entirely unwired. No ControlClient constructed.
- **Effort**: Medium

### 3.2 Format Engine Conditionals
- **Location**: `src/status/format.zig`
- **Problem**: No `#{?var,true,false}`, `#{==:a,b}`, string operations.
- **Effort**: Medium

### 3.3 Stub Command Completion
- Lock commands (lock-client/server/session): implement actual locking
- display-popup: render popup overlay
- display-menu: interactive selection UI
- confirm-before: interactive y/n via key events
- customize-mode: interactive option editor
- wait-for: blocking wait on channel
- **Effort**: Medium-High (popup/menu require overlay rendering)

### 3.4 Metal createNSString Fix
- **Location**: `src/render/metal.zig:162-174`
- **Problem**: Returns empty string instead of the passed data.
- **Effort**: Low

### 3.5 Automatic Window Renaming
- Read foreground process name from PTY, update window.name.
- **Effort**: Low

### 3.6 Extended Key Protocols (CSI u)
- Kitty keyboard protocol, xterm modifyOtherKeys.
- **Effort**: Medium

### 3.7 Pane Title (select-pane -T)
- Store title on Pane, display in status/border.
- **Effort**: Low

### 3.8 Pane Marking (select-pane -m/-M)
- Track marked pane for `{marked}` target.
- **Effort**: Low

### 3.9 DCS Passthrough
- Handle DCS events in input_handler for DECRQSS, tmux escape passthrough.
- **Effort**: Medium

### 3.10 remain-on-exit
- Detect pane exit, show "Pane is dead" overlay.
- **Effort**: Low

### 3.11 Session Groups
- Shared windows across sessions.
- **Effort**: Medium

### 3.12 OSC Buffer Size
- **Location**: `src/terminal/input.zig:79`
- Increase OSC data buffer from 256 to 65536 for clipboard payloads.
- **Effort**: Low

## Implementation Priority Order

1. UTF-8 input parsing (1.1) — unblocks all non-ASCII
2. Fix insertLines/deleteLines (2.2) — unblocks ncurses apps
3. Alt-screen buffer (1.2) — unblocks vim/less/htop
4. Missing CSI sequences (2.6) — completes terminal emulation
5. Multi-pane compositing (1.3) — core visual feature
6. Mouse event routing (1.4) — expected by users
7. Hook auto-firing (2.4) — low effort, high value
8. Layout cycling (2.3) — low effort
9. Activity/bell detection (2.5) — low effort
10. OSC buffer size (3.12) — low effort
11. Wire options store (2.1) — architectural cleanup
12. Control mode (3.1) — enables tooling integration
13. Format conditionals (3.2) — status bar power
14. Stub commands (3.3) — full command parity
15. Remaining Tier 3 items

# agentmux vs tmux: Behavioral Gap Analysis

## Summary

agentmux implements ~70 tmux-compatible commands with a full client/server architecture,
protocol, PTY management, key bindings, session/window/pane hierarchy, copy mode,
choose-tree, hooks, and terminal rendering pipeline. The build and all tests pass.

## Behavioral Gaps Found

### 1. CRITICAL: `new-session` ignores terminal size from client
**File**: `src/cmd/cmd.zig:1825`
**Issue**: `cmdNewSession` hard-codes 80x24 instead of using the actual client terminal dimensions.
**tmux behavior**: Uses the connecting client's terminal size.
**Fix**: Pass client terminal dimensions through the context or read from the identify message.

### 2. CRITICAL: `new-window` ignores terminal size
**File**: `src/cmd/cmd.zig:1893`
**Issue**: `cmdNewWindow` hard-codes 80x24 for new windows.
**tmux behavior**: Uses the session's current window size.
**Fix**: Use the session's active window dimensions (or client terminal size).

### 3. CRITICAL: `split-window` default direction is wrong
**File**: `src/cmd/cmd.zig:1915`
**Issue**: Default direction is `.horizontal` (side-by-side). tmux's default `split-window` (no flags) splits vertically (top/bottom). The `-h` flag means horizontal split (side-by-side).
**tmux behavior**: Default is vertical split (top/bottom). `-h` = horizontal (side-by-side), `-v` = vertical (top/bottom).
**Fix**: Default should be `.vertical`. The `-h` flag sets `.horizontal`, `-v` sets `.vertical`.

### 4. MODERATE: `new-session` doesn't parse `-n` (window name) or shell command
**File**: `src/cmd/cmd.zig:1815-1827`
**Issue**: Only `-s` (session name) is parsed. `-n window-name`, `-c start-directory`, `-x width`, `-y height`, and trailing shell-command are ignored.
**tmux behavior**: All these flags are functional.
**Fix**: Parse and apply `-n`, `-c`, `-x`, `-y`, and shell-command arguments.

### 5. MODERATE: `list-sessions` format doesn't match tmux output
**File**: `src/cmd/cmd.zig:2103-2111`
**Issue**: Outputs `name: N windows (attached: M)` but tmux outputs `name: N windows (created <date>)` with optional `(attached)` suffix.
**tmux behavior**: `0: 1 windows (created Fri Apr  4 12:00:00 2026) (attached)`
**Fix**: Add creation timestamp to Session struct and format output to match tmux.

### 6. MODERATE: `list-windows` format doesn't match tmux output
**File**: `src/cmd/cmd.zig` (cmdListWindows)
**Issue**: Format is `N: name [WxH] (P panes)` — tmux uses `N: name* (P panes) [WxH] [layout ...]`
**tmux behavior**: Includes active window marker `*`, layout description.
**Fix**: Add `*` marker for active window, include layout info.

### 7. MODERATE: `list-panes` format doesn't match tmux output  
**File**: `src/cmd/cmd.zig` (cmdListPanes)
**Issue**: Format differs from tmux's `%N: [WxH] [layout ...] (active)` format.
**tmux behavior**: Shows pane ID with `%` prefix, marks active pane.
**Fix**: Format pane ID as `%N`, add `(active)` marker.

### 8. MODERATE: `rename-session` doesn't parse `-t` target
**File**: `src/cmd/cmd.zig:2402-2406`
**Issue**: Always renames `ctx.session`. The `-t target-session` flag is not parsed.
**tmux behavior**: `-t` specifies which session to rename.
**Fix**: Parse `-t` and resolve target session.

### 9. MODERATE: Session doesn't track creation time
**File**: `src/session.zig`
**Issue**: No `created_at` timestamp field.
**tmux behavior**: Sessions have creation timestamps visible in `list-sessions`.
**Fix**: Add `created_at: i64` field, set via `time()` in `Session.init()`.

### 10. LOW: `lock-client`, `lock-server`, `lock-session` are stubs
**File**: `src/cmd/cmd.zig:747-757`
**Issue**: These just print messages. No actual locking mechanism.
**tmux behavior**: Locks the terminal display, requires password to unlock.
**Impact**: Low — rarely used feature.

### 11. LOW: `show-messages` is a stub
**File**: `src/cmd/cmd.zig:763-765`
**Issue**: Always prints "no messages". Server has a `messages` list but it's never displayed.
**tmux behavior**: Shows server message log.
**Fix**: Display `server.messages` entries.

### 12. LOW: `suspend-client` is a stub
**File**: `src/cmd/cmd.zig:767-769`
**Issue**: No-op. Should send SIGTSTP to client.
**tmux behavior**: Sends SIGTSTP to the client process.

### 13. LOW: `refresh-client` is a stub
**File**: `src/cmd/cmd.zig:759-761`
**Issue**: No-op. Should trigger screen redraw.
**tmux behavior**: Forces terminal redraw.

### 14. LOW: `display-popup` executes synchronously
**File**: `src/cmd/cmd.zig:3485-3515`
**Issue**: Blocks the server while popup command runs. tmux runs popup in a separate pane overlay.
**tmux behavior**: Popup is an overlay pane that doesn't block.

### 15. LOW: `confirm-before` doesn't handle interactive confirmation
**File**: `src/cmd/cmd.zig:3409-3439`
**Issue**: With `-y` flag it works. Without it, just prints the prompt but can't read response.
**tmux behavior**: Interactive prompt with key input routing.

### 16. LOW: `command-prompt` is interactive-only
**File**: `src/cmd/cmd.zig` (cmdCommandPrompt)
**Issue**: Sets up prompt state but relies on key event routing which works in interactive mode.
**tmux behavior**: Same — this is inherently interactive.

### 17. LOW: No format string expansion (#S, #W, #P, etc.)
**File**: Multiple commands
**Issue**: `-F format` flags are parsed but format strings like `#{session_name}` are not expanded.
**tmux behavior**: Full format string expansion with `#{}` syntax.
**Impact**: Affects `list-sessions -F`, `list-windows -F`, `list-panes -F`, `display-message -F`.

### 18. LOW: `wait-for` without flags can't block
**File**: `src/cmd/cmd.zig:3613-3654`
**Issue**: The bare `wait-for channel` (blocking wait) returns immediately because async I/O is needed.
**tmux behavior**: Blocks the client until the channel is signaled.

### 19. LOW: `copy-mode` search (`search_next`/`search_prev`) is a no-op
**File**: `src/cmd/cmd.zig:425-426`
**Issue**: CopyAction `.search_next` and `.search_prev` do nothing in `handleCopyModeAction`.
**tmux behavior**: Searches scrollback buffer for pattern matches.

### 20. LOW: `select-layout` doesn't support named layouts
**File**: `src/cmd/cmd.zig:1334-1355`
**Issue**: Layout name argument is ignored. Only triggers resize.
**tmux behavior**: Supports `even-horizontal`, `even-vertical`, `main-horizontal`, `main-vertical`, `tiled`.

    fn renderComposedToClient(

        const ps = pane_state_opt orelse return error.NoPaneState;

        // Use a pipe so Output can flush to the write end while we
        // collect the bytes from the read end. Set write end non-blocking
        // to prevent deadlock when output exceeds the kernel pipe buffer.
        var pipe_fds: [2]std.c.fd_t = undefined;

String:     fn drawLayoutBorders(self: *Server, out: *output_mod.Output, cell: *const @import("layout/layout.zig").LayoutCell, total_cols: u32, total_rows: u32) void {
        const LayoutCell = @import("layout/layout.zig").LayoutCell;
        const CellType = @import("layout/layout.zig").CellType;

        if (cell.cell_type == .pane) return;

        // For each pair of adjacent children, draw a border between them.
        const children = cell.children.items;
        if (children.len < 2) return;

        for (children[0 .. children.len - 1], 1..) |child, next_idx| {
            const next = children[next_idx];
            _ = next;

            if (cell.cell_type == .horizontal) {
                // Vertical border: draw at (child.xoff + child.sx, child.yoff) to (child.xoff + child.sx, child.yoff + child.sy - 1)
                const bx = child.xoff + child.sx;
                const top = child.yoff;
                const bottom = child.yoff + child.sy -| 1;
                out.attrReset();
                var y = top;
                while (y <= bottom) : (y += 1) {
                    out.cursorTo(bx, y);
                    out.writeBytes("\xe2\x94\x82"); // U+2502 u2502
                }
            } else if (cell.cell_type == .vertical) {
                // Horizontal border: draw at (child.xoff, child.yoff + child.sy) to (child.xoff + child.sx - 1, child.yoff + child.sy)
                const by = child.yoff + child.sy;
                const left = child.xoff;
                const right = child.xoff + child.sx -| 1;
                out.attrReset();
                out.cursorTo(left, by);
                var x = left;
                while (x <= right) : (x += 1) {
                    out.writeBytes("\xe2\x94\x80"); // U+2500 u2500
                }
            }
        }

        // Recurse into children.
        for (children) |child| {
            const child_cell: *const LayoutCell = @ptrCast(@alignCast(child));

            } else if (cell.cell_type == .vertical) {
                // Horizontal border: draw at (child.xoff, child.yoff + child.sy) to (child.xoff + child.sx - 1, child.yoff + child.sy)
                const by = child.yoff + child.sy;
                const left = child.xoff;
                const right = child.xoff + child.sx -| 1;
                out.attrReset();
                out.cursorTo(left, by);
                var x = left;
                while (x <= right) : (x += 1) {
                    out.writeBytes("\xe2\x94\x80"); // U+2500 u2500
                }
            }
        }

        // Recurse into children.
        for (children) |child| {
            const child_cell: *const LayoutCell = @ptrCast(@alignCast(child));
            if (child_cell.cell_type != CellType.pane) {
                self.drawLayoutBorders(out, child_cell, total_cols, total_rows);
            }
        }
    }</tool_use_error>
